title: uragoga glabrescens
side_effects: Uragoga glabrescens can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches, which may be caused by excessive consumption or incorrect preparation of the plant-based remedy. In severe cases, prolonged use of uragoga glabrescens can lead to liver damage, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of bleeding. Some individuals may also experience stomach ulcers, diarrhea, or vomiting due to the plant's high concentration of toxic compounds. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using uragoga glabrescens to ensure safe and effective treatment.
